mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that compresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization that supports near-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO within easing cycles, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values increase inflation expectations and real discount rates, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surpassing 2.5 percent annually confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside a rising federal funds rate signals a supply-demand mismatch under tightening policy, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) during tightening regimes.
